title: CoW Swap - Open App
meta_description: cow swap opens the CoW Protocol trading interface for intent-based swaps, limit orders, and TWAPs with MEV-aware execution.
hero_h2: MEV-aware token trades from the official interface
hero_lead: CoW Swap is the first trading interface built on CoW Protocol, letting users sign intents for swaps, limit orders, and TWAPs that solvers route across on-chain liquidity.
what_is: CoW Swap is the first interface built on top of CoW Protocol and a reference implementation for its intent-based trading UX. Verify that role in the official CoW Protocol docs before launching the app.

how.d: Use swap.cow.fi and check the domain before connecting a wallet.
Choose an injected wallet, Safe, WalletConnect-compatible mobile wallet, or supported hardware wallet path.
Pick the network, sell token, buy token, amount, and order type such as swap, limit, or TWAP.
Check output, expiry, slippage, and approval prompts, then sign the intent from your wallet.

points.p: Instead of sending an immediate on-chain swap transaction, users sign an intent and solvers compete to execute it through available routes.
The protocol documentation highlights MEV protection and price improvement as core benefits of the CoW Protocol model.
The app supports common wallet paths and order styles while routing through CoW Protocol's solver-based settlement design.

faq.q: Is cow swap the official app?
faq.a: The action interface is hosted at swap.cow.fi; always verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: How does cow swap execute trades?
faq.a: Users sign trade intents, and CoW Protocol solvers compete to find execution paths rather than relying only on a direct pool swap.

faq.q: Does cow swap support limit orders?
faq.a: Yes. The CoW Protocol docs list market swaps, limit orders, TWAP orders, native-token flows, and cross-chain swap options.

faq.q: What wallets work with cow swap?
faq.a: The docs mention injected wallets, Safe, WalletConnect v2 wallets, and hardware-wallet paths through supported wallet integrations.
No. Review token, network, approval, slippage, and order details yourself before signing; DeFi transactions carry execution and smart-contract risk.

footer_note: This page is not the official app; verify swap.cow.fi before connecting a wallet; educational content only, not financial advice.
